metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Draftsim Staff

set_overview.summary: Lorwyn Eclipsed is a slow, board-stall-friendly format dominated by five primary tribal archetypes: GW Kithkin, WU Merfolk, BR Goblins, UR Elementals, and BG Elves. Efficient board wipes are scarce, so most games are decided by executing your archetype's game plan. Fliers thrive on congested boards, and rummaging/looting effects help manage mana flood. BG Elves is the strongest archetype; BR Goblins struggles relative to its peers.

color_notes.Tempo: Slow format — board stalls are common; aerial pressure and convoke decks break through.
color_notes.Removal: Universal answers like Sunderflock and Ashling's Command are among the format's strongest cards.
color_notes.Tribes: Kindred matters — cards count as their listed creature type for triggered abilities. Tribal payoffs reward mono-type devotion.
color_notes.Mana flood: Manage flood with looters and rummaging effects (Flaring Cinder, mana sinks like Timid Shieldbearer and Safewright Cavalry).

key_gameplay_notes: "Lorwyn Eclipsed is the safest Limited set to dump your hand that we've ever seen" — sweepers are rare, so aggressive positioning is rewarded.
Fliers punish congested boards; aerial creatures are valuable.
Identify powerful payoff cards (bombs, tribe-specific uncommons) early, then follow supporting cards as they table.
Vivid is a strong backup plan — changelings + uncommon vivid payoffs (Kithkeeper, Rime Chill, Shimmercreep, Wildvine Pummeler) keep you flexible.
Consider 16-land builds in creature-heavy decks running convoke or mana dorks.

mechanics.name: Vivid
mechanics.summary: Some Vivid cards function as strong standalone playables; dedicated Vivid decks leverage enablers and uncommon payoffs.
mechanics.performance: Reliable both as splashable standalones and as a dedicated archetype.

mechanics.name: Blight
mechanics.summary: A support mechanic built around -1/-1 counters rather than a primary strategy. Cards like Cinder Strike and Pyrrhic Strike enable it; Moonlit Lamenter and Gnarlbark Elm are payoffs but require consistent counter outlets.
mechanics.performance: Support layer for BR/BG decks; underperforms without a dedicated outlet density.

mechanics.name: Changeling
mechanics.summary: Every creature type. Glues tribal archetypes together while staying flexible across multiple decks.
mechanics.performance: Strong universal role-players. The common changeling cycle is especially well-rated.

mechanics.name: Kindred
mechanics.summary: Tribal payoffs. Uncommon tribe-specific cards and the rare Commands cycle drive archetype construction. Cards count as their listed creature types for triggered abilities.
mechanics.performance: Defines deck identity. The five Commands are among the format's premier rares.

mechanics.name: Convoke
mechanics.summary: Synergises with wide creature strategies — particularly Merfolk — and stealth-supports elemental "4 or more" mechanics.
mechanics.performance: Powerful enabler in go-wide and tapper-heavy decks.

mechanics.name: Behold
mechanics.summary: Reveal-a-creature-from-hand effects. The uncommon cycle (including Soulbright Seeker) provides archetype-specific value; rare Champions cards are bombs.
mechanics.performance: Builds toward archetype payoffs; Champion rares are top-tier.

mechanics.name: Double-Faced Cards & Evokementals
mechanics.summary: Five legendary DFCs (one per archetype) and the Evokementals cycle. DFC quality varies by deck context; Evokementals offer modal value depending on available mana.
mechanics.performance: DFCs that match your archetype are bombs; off-archetype copies are mediocre.

key_examples.name: Brigid, Clachan's Heart // Brigid, Doun's Mind
Sygg, Wanderwine Wisdom // Sygg, Wanderbrine Shield
Grub, Storied Matriarch // Grub, Notorious Auntie
Trystan, Callous Cultivator // Trystan, Penitent Culler
Eirdu, Carrier of Dawn // Isilu, Carrier of Twilight

tier_1.name: BG Elves
tier_1.strategy: Self-mill enables your payoffs. The deepest common creature base in the format. Combine -1/-1 counter synergies with mill payoffs for a consistent value engine.

key_cards.name: Trystan, Callous Cultivator // Trystan, Penitent Culler

tier_2.name: GW Kithkin
tier_2.strategy: Aggressive midrange built on tribal payoffs. Changelings provide flexibility while keeping creature-type counts high.

key_cards.name: Brigid, Clachan's Heart // Brigid, Doun's Mind

tier_2.name: WU Merfolk
tier_2.strategy: Aggressive tempo deck leaning on convoke and wide creature development. Tappers and creatures that benefit from tapping turn on efficient spells.

key_cards.name: Sygg, Wanderwine Wisdom // Sygg, Wanderbrine Shield

tier_3.name: UR Elementals
tier_3.strategy: Tempo-focused, leveraging elemental count and "4 or more" payoffs. Looters like Flaring Cinder smooth out flood; convoke supports a wide board.

tier_3.name: Gx Vivid
tier_3.strategy: When primary archetypes are closed, pivot to a green-based Vivid deck using changelings to enable uncommon Vivid payoffs.

tier_4.name: BR Goblins
tier_4.tier: Tier 4 — Weakest of the Big Five
tier_4.strategy: Blight-focused goblin tribal. Suffers from limited payoff density and inconsistent -1/-1 counter outlets compared to peer archetypes.

key_cards.name: Grub, Storied Matriarch // Grub, Notorious Auntie

tier_4.name: UB Faeries / Flash
tier_4.strategy: Relies heavily on Voracious Tome-Skimmer. Poorly supported overall — only viable when the seat is wide open.

tier_4.name: RW Aggro
tier_4.strategy: Overlaps significantly with the primary archetypes; lacks dedicated payoffs.

tier_4.name: WB Midrange
tier_4.strategy: Overlaps significantly with the primary archetypes; lacks dedicated payoffs.

notable_rares.name: Trystan's Command
notable_rares.note: Top-rated card in the set; archetype-defining rare.

notable_rares.name: Brigid, Clachan's Heart // Brigid, Doun's Mind
Sygg, Wanderwine Wisdom // Sygg, Wanderbrine Shield
Grub, Storied Matriarch // Grub, Notorious Auntie
Ashling, Rekindled // Ashling, Rimebound
notable_rares.note: DFC — needs blue mana for full value.

notable_rares.name: Trystan, Callous Cultivator // Trystan, Penitent Culler
notable_rares.note: DFC — playable in any green deck; excellent in BG Elves.

notable_rares.name: Eirdu, Carrier of Dawn // Isilu, Carrier of Twilight
Slumbering Walker
notable_rares.note: Common rare overperformer at common rarity.

draft_plan.pack_1: Take the most powerful card available. Identify a payoff card (bomb or tribe-specific uncommon) early and let it anchor your archetype choice. Universal removal (Sunderflock, Ashling's Command, Cinder Strike) is always strong. Don't lock in two colors before pack 1 ends.
draft_plan.pack_2: Commit to your archetype based on signals. Pick supporting tribal pieces and the on-color Commands or DFCs aggressively. Note open colors for a pivot if signals are bad — Vivid is your best backup plan.
draft_plan.pack_3: Fill out your curve and shore up weaknesses. Prioritize 2-drops, additional removal, and fliers to beat board stalls. Take changelings late if your tribal count is low.

general_tips: Sweepers are rare — feel safe dumping your hand to apply pressure.
Fliers and reach matter; board stalls are common.
Tribal payoffs reward mono-type devotion; track your creature-type counts.
Looters and rummagers (e.g., Flaring Cinder) hedge against mana flood.
Consider 16-land builds when running convoke or many mana sinks.
Universal removal (Sunderflock, Ashling's Command) is the strongest pick-up at most points.
If your primary archetype is closed, pivot to a Vivid deck using changelings + uncommon Vivid payoffs.
